key_themes: Robust Q1 2026 operational momentum (202 leases, 286 total assets transacted)
Aggressive capital returns ($745M share repurchases in Q1 + dividend hike)
Persistent aircraft supply shortage supporting lease rates and asset values
Bullish analyst coverage with repeated price target increases ($160–$175)
Attractive valuation (low P/E, ~1.2x P/B) following record 2025 results
summary: AerCap (AER) is seeing strongly positive sentiment in mid-2026. The company reported very busy Q1 activity—including 202 lease agreements across aircraft/engines/helicopters, dozens of purchases and sales with gains, and approximately $745 million in share repurchases—while benefiting from a structural global aircraft shortage that keeps values and lease rates elevated.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/aercap-reports-286-transactions-3-112736037.html)[[2]](https://x.com/andrewcoye/status/2040038781957398854) Following record 2025 earnings beats, dividend increases, and buybacks, Wall Street analysts remain overwhelmingly bullish with multiple upward revisions to price targets ($160–$175 range) and predominantly Buy/Strong Buy ratings, citing shareholder-friendly capital allocation and still-undervalued multiples around 1.2x estimated book value. Q1 2026 financial results are scheduled for release April 29. Mild caution around conservative full-year guidance is outweighed by execution and market tailwinds.[[3]](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/AER)[[4]](https://uk.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/td-cowen-raises-aercap-stock-price-target-on-strong-sales-activity-93CH-4604970)
sources: AerCap official releases (Q1 2026 activity report, Q1 earnings call announcement, AGM), 10+ analyst notes & articles (TipRanks, Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, TD Cowen, Morgan Stanley, Deutsche Bank), ~10 relevant recent X/investor posts discussing leases, buybacks, and valuation
